w:r.w:t: MECHANICAL DESIGNER • SOLIDWORKS • MACHINE SHOP • SHEET METAL
BOMs, exploded views, revision control, standards
Configurable platforms, reuse of common components, options management, drawing/BOM variants
Basic Python/JavaScript, internal tools to streamline BOM work, drawing
Mechanical Designer with 15+ years of experience in 3D modeling, 2D fabrication drawings, and BOM management for custom, design-to-order industrial equipment. Strong

background in SolidWorks, sheet metal, structural frames, and production documentation that shop and fabrication teams can trust. Experienced in building multi-variant product families from common components (DTO) and standardizing designs for manufacturability. Known
automation to reduce errors and lead times while keeping designs practical and buildable.
Design complete pump packages, engine mounts, sheet-metal components, lifting structures, vacuum systems, and chassis in SolidWorks within a design-to-order
Configure base platforms into customer-specific variants (models, engines, control panels, trailer options), then deliver accurate 3D models, 2D fabrication drawings, and BOMs for each order.
Produce fabrication drawings, DXFs, and flat patterns for laser cutting and brake forming, with clear datums, weld symbols, and tolerances.
Create and maintain accurate Bills of Materials (BOMs) for complex assemblies; standardize naming, part numbering, and revision practices to reduce errors across
and workflow diagnostics—reducing manual rework and bottlenecks.
Designed pneumatic planetary reduction gearboxes, handle assemblies, and related hardware for industrial torque tools across multiple product lines each with several torque/output/RPM variants.
design control for new DTO variants in the
Created detailed 3D models, 2D assembly drawings, exploded views, and tolerance- controlled prints to support repeatable production.
documentation, clarifying critical dimensions, and aligning designs with shop capabilities.
Created the tools and databases used to manage projects from
through production: scheduling, materials, vendor coordination, and production paperwork.
Used 3D scanning and tools like Geomagic to reverse-engineer real-world geometry into accurate, parametric CAD models.
to track job status, parts, and throughput across machining and assembly.
